signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Jobs Data
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.8732 — NZD leads G8 performance while CAD lags following softer-than-expected Canadian employment data. RBNZ hold at 2.25% with a hike bias vs BoC 2.25% on hold creates a hawkish divergence in forward guidance. The 1.564% G8 spread indicates a strong bilateral theme as CAD faces growth headwinds while NZD benefits from spec positioning recovery. Watch for the RBNZ meeting on 27 May to confirm the hawkish path.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (specs fading hawkishness) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the next meeting. With NZD/USD at 0.5967 and the pair gaining +1.01% on the week, a crowded spec short unwind risk is elevated if RBNZ delivers a hawkish surprise. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% maintains a +150bp USD carry advantage, but the OIS-positioning divergence suggests the market is under-positioned for NZD strength. Watch for a break of 0.6000 to trigger further short covering.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7251 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 88% short. This extreme institutional vs retail divergence at the top of a 1W +0.69% move suggests retail is aggressively fading the trend. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp AUD carry premium, supporting the institutional long case. Retail capitulation risk rises if price extends toward 0.7300, potentially accelerating the move as shorts are squeezed. Watch for RBA meeting on 16 Jun for policy confirmation.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.67 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite reports of potential US-Iran talks. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 remains crowded, leaving the pair vulnerable to sharp unwinds if VIX (currently 17.2) spikes above 25. While the MOVE index fell -6.90% to 67.3, indicating bond market calm, the 160.00 level remains the critical threshold for BoJ intervention risk. Carry dominates the regime until geopolitical escalation triggers a sustained safe-haven bid.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + ECB Stance
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.719 vs norm of -0.125 (z=+1.78σ) — the pair is moving in lockstep with European equities, breaking its historical inverse relationship. ECB's Lagarde warns of higher energy costs pushing up input prices, yet EUR/USD holds 1.1790. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% maintains a +175bp USD carry advantage, structurally limiting upside. Watch for the ECB meeting on 11 Jun to see if inflation warnings shift the 'on hold' bias.
